content: I'm still getting used to living in Berlin, Germany. It's been a big adjustment, but I'm loving the city so far. My border collie, Thor, is adjusting well too.
I'm sure it can be challenging to adjust to a new city, but it's great that you're enjoying it. What do you like most about Berlin so far?

I think my favorite thing is the food - I'm a big fan of German cuisine, and there are so many great restaurants and cafes here. I've also been getting into drone photography, which has been a lot of fun.
That's great! German food is definitely delicious. As an Environmental Policy Advisor, do you find that your work is influenced by your new surroundings?

Yeah, definitely. Being in a new city has given me a fresh perspective on my work, and I've been able to connect with some really interesting people in the field. I've also been trying to get more into hiking and outdoor activities, which has been great for my mental and physical health.

That's great to hear. It's always important to prioritize self-care and make time for activities that bring you joy. Have you thought about getting involved in any local environmental initiatives or organizations?

Actually, I've been looking into some local groups that focus on sustainability and conservation. I'm thinking of volunteering with a group that does clean-up initiatives in the city's parks and waterways. And I've been baking a lot of sourdough bread, which has been a fun hobby.
That's a great way to get involved in your community and make a positive impact. Do you have any fun plans for the upcoming holidays?

Yeah, I'm thinking of hosting a dinner party for some friends and colleagues. I've been experimenting with some new recipes, and I'm excited to share them with others. Maybe we'll even have a little aquarium-themed party, since I've been getting into aquariums lately.
That sounds like a great idea! You'll have to be sure to send out some fun invitations and decorations. Have you thought about what you'll do after the holidays?

I'm actually thinking of taking a trip to the Alps in January. I've heard great things about the hiking and skiing, and I'm excited to explore a new part of Europe. And I've been trying to get more into calligraphy, which has been a fun creative outlet.
That sounds like an amazing trip! You'll have to be sure to pack plenty of warm clothing and layers. Have you thought about how you'll balance your work and travel plans?
Yeah, I've been thinking about that. I'm planning to take some time off from work and focus on my trip and personal projects. Maybe I'll even do some writing or reflection while I'm on vacation.

That's a great plan. It's always important to prioritize self-care and make time for your own interests and passions. I'm sure you'll have a great time on your trip and come back refreshed and rejuvenated.
